examples.title: Node Structure for Threaded Binary Tree
examples.description: Defines the node structure for a double-threaded binary tree with tag fields
examples.explanation: Each node has 5 fields. The tag fields indicate whether the corresponding pointer is a child link (1) or a thread (0). This structure allows the tree to store both child pointers and traversal threads in the same fields.

examples.title: Right-Inorder Threading Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to convert a binary tree to right-inorder threaded form
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ThreadInorder(root)
BEGIN
    prev ← NULL
    IF root = NULL THEN
        RETURN
    ENDIF
    // Recurse on left subtree
    ThreadInorder(root.left)
    
    // Process current node
    IF root.left = NULL THEN
        root.left ← prev      // Thread to predecessor
        root.ltag ← 0         // Mark as thread
    ELSE
        root.ltag ← 1         // Mark as child
    ENDIF
    
    IF prev = NULL THEN
        // First node - no predecessor
    ELSE
        IF prev.right = NULL THEN
            prev.right ← root  // Thread to successor
            prev.rtag ← 0      // Mark as thread
        ELSE
            prev.rtag ← 1      // Mark as child
        ENDIF
    ENDIF
    
    prev ← root
    
    // Recurse on right subtree
    ThreadInorder(root.right)
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This inorder traversal threads each node to its predecessor (left thread) and successor (right thread). The prev variable tracks the previously visited node. After execution, all null left pointers point to inorder predecessors and null right pointers point to inorder successors.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursion stack, O(1) for auxiliary variables

examples.title: Inorder Traversal of Threaded Binary Tree
examples.description: Traverses a right-inorder threaded tree without using stack or recursion
examples.explanation: This traversal starts at the leftmost node (inorder first), then follows threads to visit subsequent nodes. When a node has an actual right child (rtag=1), we go to that child and find its leftmost descendant. This achieves O(n) time with O(1) space.

examples.title: Morris Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Morris traversal achieves O(1) space by temporarily creating threads
examples.code:
PROCEDURE MorrisInorder(root)
BEGIN
    current ← root
    
    WHILE current ≠ NULL DO
        IF current.left = NULL THEN
            // No left subtree - visit and go right
            VISIT(current)
            current ← current.right
        ELSE
            // Find inorder predecessor
            pre ← current.left
            WHILE pre.right ≠ NULL AND pre.right ≠ current DO
                pre ← pre.right
            ENDWHILE
            
            IF pre.right = NULL THEN
                // Create temporary thread back to current
                pre.right ← current
                current ← current.left
            ELSE
                // Thread exists - we came here via thread
                // Remove the thread and visit current
                pre.right ← NULL
                VISIT(current)
                current ← current.right
            ENDIF
        ENDIF
    ENDWHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Morris traversal uses the empty right pointers of inorder predecessors to create temporary threads back to the current node. This allows us to return to the current node after visiting the left subtree. After visiting each node, the thread is removed to restore the tree structure. No extra space is needed for stack or recursion.
